description: Cape Verdean organized crime refers to criminal organizations active in Cape Verde and its diaspora, primarily involved in drug trafficking as a transshipment point for cocaine from South America to Europe and the United States. These groups are largely street gangs with varying organization, operating in Cape Verdean communities in New England, Rotterdam, Portugal, France, and Switzerland. They engage in additional crimes such as weapon trafficking, prostitution, and money laundering.

country_links.evidence_quote: Cape Verdean gangs are active in the southeast of Belgium, namely in the province of Luxembourg. A well-known power base is the town of Athus, where they conduct criminal activities such drug trafficking as well as prostitution.
country_links.context: Cape Verdean gangs are active in southeast Belgium, conducting drug trafficking and prostitution.

country_links.evidence_quote: Cape Verdean communities exist in several Parisian suburbs. Some of them are home to their own local gangs. Several of the gangs are active in the large-scale importation, as well as the wholesale and retail distribution of narcotics...
country_links.context: Cape Verdean gangs in Parisian suburbs are active in large-scale importation and distribution of narcotics.

country_links.evidence_quote: In the Netherlands, Cape Verdeans have the biggest presence in the port city of Rotterdam. ... some Cape Verdean criminals are active alongside established Dutch Antillean criminal organizations. Their criminal activities mostly concern large narcotics importations, local drug distribution, money laundering and murder.
country_links.context: Cape Verdean criminals in Rotterdam are active in narcotics importation, drug distribution, money laundering, and murder.

country_links.evidence_quote: Cape Verdean gangs are one of the most important sources of drugs in Portugal. The notorious Lisbon neighborhood Cova da Moura, with a large Cape Verdean community, is a known stage for local rival criminal organizations battling over the drug trade...
country_links.context: Cape Verdean gangs are a major source of drugs in Portugal, operating in Lisbon's Cova da Moura neighborhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: The small Swiss city of Martigny is home to two main criminal organizations, one Albanian mafia gang and another gang composed of persons of Cape Verdean descent, which have traditionally rivaled each other over the monopoly in trafficking of narcotics and other criminal activities...
country_links.context: A Cape Verdean gang in Martigny rivals an Albanian mafia gang over narcotics trafficking and other crimes.

country_links.evidence_quote: The New England region in the United States, especially the cities of Boston and Brockton in Massachusetts and the Rhode Island state, is home to a sizeable Cape Verdean community. Several of the region's neighborhoods are home to street gangs. ... some others are involved in large-scale drug trafficking enterprises, as well as extortion, money laundering...
country_links.context: Cape Verdean street gangs in New England and North Carolina are involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering.
